overview: The Wausau Board of Public Works met on June 16, 2026, approving several contracts and bids including a $303,888 award for East Parking Ramp repairs, a $578,572.35 pay estimate for lead service line replacement, and a change order for the downtown utility project. The board moved through six action items before entering closed session to consider pre-qualification statements for the North Fourth Street Lighting Project.
presiding: Name not clearly identified in transcript

agenda.item: Approval of minutes from June 9, 2026 meeting
Item 2A: Open bids and recommendation for 2026 East Parking Ramp Project repairs
Item 2B: Pay estimate #28 for Community Infrastructure Partners - lead service line replacement
Item 2C: 2026 Downtown Utility Project - Haas Inc. Change Order #1
Item 2D: Amendment 1 to contract with WisDOT and MSA Professional Services for Business Campus Trail East-West Connector
Item 2E: Portland Cement Concrete License for Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC and B&D Plumbing and Heating Inc.
Item 2F: Bituminous Concrete Paving License for Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC
Item 3: Closed session pursuant to Wisconsin Statutes 19.85.1E - pre-qualification statements for 2026 North Fourth Street Lighting Project

discussions.body: A board member moved to approve the minutes as drafted, seconded by another member. The motion passed unanimously on a voice vote.

discussions.item: 2026 East Parking Ramp Project repairs - bid award
discussions.body: Six bids were received and read into the record. The low bid of $303,888 from Masonry Restorations Inc. was presented, with the next lowest being Building Restoration Corporation at $337,186. A board member noted the project budget was $300,000, making the low bid $3,888 over budget but within acceptable range. The board voted unanimously to award the contract to Masonry Restorations Inc. at $303,888.

discussions.item: Pay estimate #28 - Community Infrastructure Partners, lead service line replacement
discussions.body: A board member referenced an email from staff member Eric recommending approval, noting the work had been completed and inspected and that retainage was being withheld for any punch list items. The board voted unanimously to approve pay estimate #28 in the amount of $578,572.35.

discussions.item: 2026 Downtown Utility Project - Haas Inc. Change Order #1
discussions.body: Staff member TJ explained the change order resulted from upgrading a water main from 8-inch to 12-inch diameter after the bid, determined to benefit the water system, and a larger sanitary manhole core required because the line was found to be 18-inch rather than the 15-inch shown in historical records. The net increase to the contract is $17,433.12 ($50,791.48 added minus $33,358.36 reduced). A board member confirmed the water department requested the upsizing and that the project is TID funded. The board voted unanimously to approve the change order.

discussions.item: Amendment 1 - Business Campus Trail East-West Connector contract (City of Wausau, WisDOT, MSA Professional Services)
discussions.body: TJ explained that a property owner along the planned trail route made an unreasonable purchase request and the city lacked eminent domain authority, requiring a trail redesign. The amendment totals $15,795.25, of which the city is responsible for only 20% due to 80/20 federal TAP program funding, though the full amount must be paid out before reimbursement. The board voted unanimously to approve Amendment 1.

discussions.item: Portland Cement Concrete License - Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC and B&D Plumbing and Heating Inc.
discussions.body: A board member moved to approve the license for both companies subject to their further personal review, noting they had not fully reviewed the materials before the meeting and had a question on the first applicant. The motion was seconded and passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Bituminous Concrete Paving License - Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC
discussions.body: A board member moved to approve the bituminous concrete paving license for Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC, also subject to their final review. The motion was seconded and passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Closed session - pre-qualification statements for 2026 North Fourth Street Lighting Project
discussions.body: The board voted unanimously to enter closed session pursuant to Wisconsin Statutes 19.85.1E to consider and deliberate on pre-qualification statements for the 2026 North Fourth Street Lighting Project. The motion was seconded and passed on a voice vote.

actionItems: Award 2026 East Parking Ramp Project repairs contract to Masonry Restorations Inc. at $303,888
Approve and sign pay estimate #28 for Community Infrastructure Partners in the amount of $578,572.35 (signatures deferred to end of meeting)
Approve Change Order #1 for Haas Inc. on the 2026 Downtown Utility Project increasing contract by $17,433.12
Approve Amendment 1 to contract with WisDOT and MSA Professional Services for Business Campus Trail East-West Connector at $15,795.25 total increase
Approve Portland Cement Concrete Licenses for Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC and B&D Plumbing and Heating Inc. pending board member's final review
Approve Bituminous Concrete Paving License for Utility Traffic and Restoration LLC pending board member's final review
Enter closed session to consider pre-qualification statements for 2026 North Fourth Street Lighting Project
title: Wausau Board of Public Works Meeting Pt.1 - 6/16/26
